title: American Factories Lag in Adopting A.I. This Drugmaker Is an Exception.
summary: A Bristol Myers Squibb plant that makes cancer drugs was the only manufacturer in the U.S. recognized for innovation by the World Economic Forum this year.

title: Jeeves and Ask.com Shut Down After Almost 30 Years
summary: The pioneering search engine shut down on May 1, after nearly 30 years in operation.

title: These Countries Embrace E.V.s to Avoid Oil Price Shocks
summary: People in Costa Rica and other Latin American, Asian and African countries are increasingly buying electric vehicles to avoid spiking fuel prices.

title: Elon Musk Wanted OpenAI to Go Commercial, Greg Brockman Testifies
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president, testified in a trial pitting Mr. Musk against his company that the world’s richest man was eager to change how it operated as a nonprofit.

title: Apple Reaches $250 Million Settlement Over Claims It Misled People on A.I.
summary: Some iPhone owners will be eligible to receive $25 to $95 over claims that the tech giant oversold its artificial intelligence system, Apple Intelligence.

title: Coinbase Lays Off 14% of Employees as A.I. Changes Work
summary: The largest U.S. crypto exchange said it was cutting jobs because of cryptocurrency market volatility and to “optimize” for the artificial intelligence era.

title: Ford Says an Affordable Electric Pickup Truck is Still Coming Next Year
summary: Ford Motor has written off $20 billion in electric vehicle investments but says it is forging ahead with an electric pickup that will sell for $30,000 next year.

title: Anthropic and Wall Street Giants Join Forces to Create New A.I. Firm
summary: Blackstone and Goldman Sachs are among the investors in the new firm, which will help integrate Anthropic’s A.I. model Claude into their systems.

title: S.E.C. Settles Lawsuit Against Elon Musk Over His Twitter Disclosures
summary: The agency, which has been pulling back on lawsuits against major companies, ended a case that had accused Mr. Musk of hiding his purchases of Twitter stock. He agreed to pay $1.5 million.

title: White House Considers Vetting A.I. Models Before They Are Released
summary: The Trump administration, which took a noninterventionist approach to artificial intelligence, is now discussing imposing oversight on A.I. models before they are made publicly available.

title: Elon Musk’s Lawyers Ask OpenAI’s President Why He Is Worth $30 Billion
summary: The legal team implied in a federal trial that Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president and co-founder, was driven by greed rather than building safe A.I.

title: Driving Electric in Costa Rica Is Surprisingly Doable
summary: The charging network is spotty, but it’s a small country.

title: How Running Shoes Have Evolved, From Ancient Greece to a Record-Breaking Marathon Time
summary: The race to near-weightlessness has been a driving force of innovation in running sneakers and helped lead to records shattering at the London Marathon.

title: Musk vs. Altman: What Is This Really About?
summary: In a landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI’s Sam Altman, the origins of OpenAI are being examined. The Times’s technology reporter Cade Metz explains what’s behind it all.

title: Why So Many People Already Own Shares of Elon Musk’s SpaceX
summary: Even before the rocket company holds a major initial public offering, many people own stock in it through so-called special purpose vehicles.

summary: The agreements with the technology companies come amid the Defense Department’s dispute with Anthropic.

title: Elon Musk’s A.I. Claims of Danger Face Limits in OpenAI Trial

title: Apple Reports 17% Sales Jump, Powered by iPhones
